caption: The charge is now physically arrested by soldier-level contact, not just a flat regiment-circle hard-block rule. A single Infantry regiment spawns already in Shield Wall and is issued Ctrl+H (Hold) immediately, so it receives the charge stationary instead of closing distance on its own; a lone Cavalry regiment then takes it head-on. Watch the cavalry visibly decelerate as their soldier bodies collide with the braced line's bodies (SoldierEnemyContact.accumulate, a mass-weighted momentum impulse) -- the charge settles at contact instead of a scripted stop. Root cause: soldier-level enemy collision physics didn't exist before this PR outside a landed melee strike, so nothing but the regiment circle's flat push_share rule stood between a charge and interpenetration.
